requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must be age 70½ or older to transfer up to $105,000 (2024) directly from traditional IRA to qualified charity as QCD, satisfying required minimum distributions while excluding amount from taxable income providing tax benefit even for non-itemizers
criteria: Must be age 70½ or older on date of distribution (can do before RMD age of 73/75)
2024 limit: $105,000 per person per year (indexed for inflation annually)
Distribution must transfer directly from IRA trustee to qualified 501(c)(3) public charity
QCD counts toward required minimum distribution but excluded from taxable income
Provides tax benefit even if take standard deduction (unlike regular charitable contributions requiring itemizing)
Reduces AGI helping avoid Medicare IRMAA, Social Security taxation, and other income-based thresholds
Can use traditional IRA, inherited IRA; cannot use SEP-IRA or SIMPLE IRA if currently receiving contributions
Cannot receive any goods or services in exchange; must be pure charitable contribution

qcd_strategy_benefits.agi_exclusion: Amount excluded from income; better than deduction as reduces AGI directly
qcd_strategy_benefits.rmd_satisfaction: QCD counts toward RMD requirement but not included in income
qcd_strategy_benefits.standard_deduction_benefit: Provides tax benefit even taking standard deduction; no need to itemize
qcd_strategy_benefits.medicare_irmaa: Lower AGI helps avoid or reduce Medicare premium surcharges (IRMAA)
qcd_strategy_benefits.social_security: Reduced AGI may decrease taxation of Social Security benefits
qcd_strategy_benefits.annual_limit_2024: $105,000 per person; married couples can each do $105,000 = $210,000 household

restrictions: Must be age 70½ or older; six-month waiting period strictly enforced (not just turn 70)
Cannot use to private foundations, donor-advised funds, or supporting organizations
Cannot receive membership benefits, tickets, or goods/services in exchange for QCD
Distribution must go directly from IRA to charity; cannot take distribution then contribute
No charitable deduction allowed for QCD amount (already excluded from income; can't double-dip)
Must receive contemporaneous written acknowledgment from charity like any charitable contribution
Cannot make QCD from 401(k), 403(b), or other retirement plans; must be IRA
If make QCD and also regular IRA contribution, QCD tax benefit reduced by deductible contribution amount
